title: Smoky Briny Peruvian Grapes Picarones
description: A smoky, briny twist on traditional Peruvian picarones, using marinated grapes in a whole-wheat dough and grilled over mesquite smoke, served with a citrusy glaze.
instructions: In a bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and a pinch of sugar; let sit for 5 minutes until foamy.
Add whole wheat flour, olive oil, and sea salt flakes to the yeast mixture and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, then cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Meanwhile, gently press the halved grapes to release some juice and mix them with mesquite wood chips in a small bowl.
After the dough has risen, fold the grape-mesquite mixture into the dough, shaping it into small balls.
Heat a griddle over medium heat and lightly oil it; place the dough balls and cook until bubbles form, then flip and grill until both sides are golden.
Transfer the cooked picarones to a platter and drizzle with lime juice and agave syrup.
Serve hot, garnished with extra sea salt flakes and a few fresh grape halves.
